response.justification: While the candidate brings strong general software engineering credentials and is currently working on AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, they fall significantly short of the core requirement of 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions (only ~13 months of AI-focused experience). Critical gaps include no listed Python expertise despite it being a required language, and no demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or unstructured data pipelines—all essential for this role.
strengths: 20 years of proven software engineering experience with current staff-level title demonstrating technical leadership
Currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development at re:cinq, directly aligned with role responsibilities
Strong expertise in TypeScript, AWS cloud infrastructure, and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Only ~13 months of AI-related experience versus 8+ years required; extensive background is in general software engineering, not AI solutions
Python not listed in technical skills despite being explicitly required; primary language experience is TypeScript and legacy languages (DLang, C/C++)
No demonstrated expertise in vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance metrics, or unstructured data pipelines—all listed as required
